user_message: Can you pull the context on VanArsdel Ltd? I need to map the stakeholders.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 17:54 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 16:44 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 15:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:23:00] Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:27:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] Michelle (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-A trap (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-A failure mode (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-A -- Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)
